texts.full_text: mean traditional presses? I think there’ something in the air right now. It feels like people are tired of waiting for an agent who will then wait months upon years before passing their work along — if they ever do at all. We need a new paradigm; we’re not going back, so what’s next? I know that my writing has changed as well: where once it was about getting published and having the book in hand (and on shelves), now there’ an element of wanting to be read. Yes yes — you can have one without t'other but if a writer is only interested because he or she wants his/her work out, then why not go directly for that? I think we’re at point where writers are getting more comfortable with the idea (and practice) self-publishing; I know it’s something many of us in NWIW have discussed. But what does this mean? Where do you draw lines — if any – between “self” and traditional publishing, especially when they’r becoming less distinct? What about marketing or distribution channels: how would that work for a group like ours who are all over the map (literally) geographically speaking. I don’t know what’s next. I think we’re going to have some fun finding out though! However, there are numerous challenges that hinder the implementation and success of these programs: 1. Demographic Factors: The aging population is growing rapidly in Kazakhstan, which increases the number of people at risk for injuries related to falls, domestic accidents, and traffic incidents. This demographic shift puts additional pressure on healthcare services and resources. 2. Economic Constraints: Limited financial resources allocated towards public health initiatives can restrict the development and implementation of comprehensive injury prevention programs specifically targeting elderly individuals in Kazakhstan. Budgetary constraints often lead to prioritizing other immediate medical needs over preventive measures, which are seen as less urgent but equally important for long-term wellbeing. 3. texts.full_text: we go in willfully, ready for the truth this film insinuates is too painful to tell. The movie opens with Hazel telling us that she has cancer (she calls it "the thing"), and then her mother tells Gus about how bad things are going when he comes over after meeting at a support group. The two teens bond quickly as they share their stories of being sick, but the film doesn't shy away from showing them in pain. The movie is based on Green's novel that was published last year to great acclaim and sales numbers (it has sold more than 3 million copies). It tells us right up front what we're getting into. The book opens with Hazel telling readers about her cancer, too: "I'm a terminal case." Green told NPR in an interview earlier this month how he came upon the idea for his novel. "I was sitting on my couch one day and I had just finished reading 'The Perks of Being A Wallflower,' which is another book that's very much set inside teenagers' heads," Green said. "And it occurred to me, you know what would be a really interesting thing? To write about kids who are dying." green told NPR he was inspired by the story his parents had shared with him when they were first dating: His mother came down sick and nearly died from an infection that wasn't diagnosed until she'd been in bed for weeks. She spent months recovering, but Green said it made her realize how precious life is. "I think I'm a pretty happy person," he told NPR's Melissa Block earlier this month about

The shield type tunneling machine must be driven forward along a predetermined linear path by a thrust of the thrust jack. However, the shield type tunneling machines suffers from a variation in tunneling resistance due to a geological change, tending to divert from a predetermined linear path. A direction of moving shield type tunneling machine thus must be rectified. The tunneling machine in general is equipped with plural direction-adjusting jacks for rectifying a direction of moving shield type tunneling machine, and the outputs of respective direction-adjusting jacks are changed so as to rectify a direction of moving shield tunneling machine. These direction-adjusting jacks, require precise control mechanisms due to their critical role in ensuring that the pipe-jacking process follows its intended linear path. However, traditional methods often face challenges such as manual operation complexities and delayed responses when adjusting directions. In existing systems: - Direction adjustment is typically done manually or through semi-automatic means. - The response time for correcting deviations can be slow due to delays between detection of deviation from the desired course and actual application by direction-adjusting jacks, leading potentially to off-course tunnel construction which requires costly corrections later on in project timelines. To address these issues: A remote-controlled pipe-jacking apparatus is proposed that integrates advanced automation technologies. This system would include sensors for real-time monitoring (e.g., inclinometers), a control unit capable of analyzing data and sending commands, as well as actuators to adjust the direction-adjusting jacks accordingly without human intervention. This invention aims at improving accuracy in pipe-jacking operations by providing an automated correction mechanism that can respond instantly upon detecting deviations from planned paths. texts.full_text: vs. steric hindrance: which is stronger? If we talk about a series of molecules: ethene, prop-1-ene, but-2-ene, etc., we see both hyperconjugation (which increases stability) and steric hindrance (which decreases stability). So which one is more powerful i.e. which of these molecules will be most stable? A: It's a contradict to explain the stability of substituted alkenes by using hyperconjugation and steric hinderance both. I think, it is more better to consider heat of hydrogenation factor in case for stability of substituted alkenes. The stability of substituted alkenes is explained by Hyperconjugation factor while the stability of geometrical alkenes (cis-trans) is explained by steric hinderance. The stability of alkene can be determined by measuring the amount of energy associated with the hydrogenation of the molecule. Since the double bond is breaking in this reaction, the energy released in hydrogenation is proportional to the energy in the double bond of the molecule. This is a useful tool because heats of hydrogenation can be measured very accurately. The ΔHo is usually around -30 kcal/mol for alkenes. Stability is simply a measure of energy. Lower energy molecules are more stable. The molecule with the lowest energy, and thus the most stable, would be the one with the highest degree of hyperconjugation, which in this case is but-2-ene. Hyperconjugation is a more dominant factor

texts.full_text: List? 500 Is Marketo ABM Workspace specific? No. Named Accounts are visible to all the Workspaces. But Lead Partition rules are still honored. Which means you can see a named account in multiple Workspaces but depending on the Lead Partition rules, you can only see leads belonging to the corresponding Workspace within that named account. Which lead attributes are used for Lead-to-Account Matching? It is based on 3 lead attributes: Email Domain, IP Address and Company Name. We convert Email Domain and IP address to the Company Names and match all 3 to identify strong and weak matches. How are strong lead matches and weak lead matches different? Strong lead matches are the leads that are directly associated with the Named Account and are matched with the Company Name. Weak lead matches are the leads that are associated with the Named Account but are matched with the Email Domain and IP Address. These leads are considered less directly linked to the Named Account. This differentiation is important as it helps to identify the strength of the relationship between the lead and the Named Account. texts.full_text: of prophylactic dexmedetomidine in preventing postoperative junctional ectopic tachycardia in pediatric cardiac surgery patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis. Junctional ectopic tachycardia is a serious tachyarrhythmic complication following pediatric cardiac surgery. It is difficult to manage and is associated with morbidity and mortality. Conventional nonpharmacological and pharmacological measures have shown limited effects. Dexmedetomidine is an α2 agonist which has recently been shown in multiple studies to be effective. The aim of this systematic review with meta-analysis was to evaluate the efficacy of prophylactic dexmedetomidine administration in the prevention of junctional ectopic tachycardia in pediatric patients following cardiac surgeries. We searched MEDLINE, EMBASE, Cochrane, Web of Science, and relevant references for studies that investigated the use of prophylactic dexmedetomidine in preventing postoperative junctional ectopic tachycardia in pediatric cardiac surgery patients. The primary outcome was the incidence of junctional ectopic tachycardia. Secondary outcomes included the duration of intensive care unit (ICU) stay, ventilation time, and the need for additional antiarrhythmic medications. We included randomized controlled trials (RCTs) and observational studies that compared the incidence of junctional ectopic tachycardia in pediatric cardiac surgery patients who received prophylactic dexmedetomidine with those who did not. The search yielded a total of 567 studies, of which 12 studies met the inclusion criteria. These studies included a total of 1,432 patients, with 742 patients receiving prophylactic dexmedetomidine and 690 patients serving as controls. How about we head off in a different direction this time, one that doesn't center around California? This time to the capital of the great Commonwealth of Virginia — Richmond. Back in October, I drove to Richmond which meant I could stop off at State Line Liquors along the way. Now, many of you, I'm betting, are veterans of this high-quality retail beer shop, jus...t like me! Located right on US-1 near Fredericksburg, it's a must-stop for any serious craft beer enthusiast. As I was browsing through their aisles in October, my mind began wandering back to Richmond itself – its rich history, vibrant culture, and of course, a thriving food scene that's as eclectic as it is delicious. And what better way to experience this city than by exploring all that craft beer has to offer? Here is a very of the key elements of FERPA, as excerpted from the U.S. Department of Education website: FERPA gives parents certain rights with respect to their education records. These rights transfer to the student when he or she reaches the age of 18 or attends a school beyond the high school level. Students to whom the rights have transferred are students." Parents or eligible students have the right to inspect and review the student's education records maintained by the school. Schools are not required to provide copies of records unless, for reasons as great distance, it is impossible for parents or eligible students to review the records. Schools may charge a fee for copies. Parents or eligible students have the right to request that a school correct records which they believe to be inaccurate or misleading. If the school decides not to amend the record, the parent or eligible student then has the right to a formal hearing. After the hearing, if the school still decides not to amend the record, the parent or eligible student has the right to place a statement with the record setting forth his or her view about the contested information. texts.full_text: ich has an ejection amount equal to or larger than the lower limit value is used, the ejection amount of the print head used is corrected using a tone correction table to be equivalent to that of a print head whose ejection amount is the lower limit value. FIG. 24 is a block diagram showing the arrangement of a printer driver 1300 (color processing unit) which runs on a host, and an ink-jet printer 1400. R, G, and B image signals (300 dpi, 8 bits per color) input to a printer driver 1300 are converted into C, M, Y, and K signals (300 dpi, 8 bits per color) by a 3D lookup table (3DLUT) of an RGB/CMYK converter 1301. Note that a process for a 300-dpi, 8-bit cyan (C) signal will be explained below, but the same applies to those for other color component signals. The 300-dpi, 8-bit (256-valued) cyan (C) signal undergoes tone correction in a tone correction unit 1302 and is expanded to 12 bits (4081 values). In this case, a tone correction table used by the tone correction unit 1302 is selected from a tone correction table database 1305 on the basis of independently input ejection amount information of the print head. The expanded 300-dpi, 12-bit C signal undergoes pseudo halftoning by a multi-valued error diffusion processor 1303 to be converted into a 300-dpi, 3-bit (5-valued) multi-valued dot image. texts.full_text: Andhra Pradesh. Āla in Telugu and Sanskrit means "Breed cattle" and Ūri means "Belonging to the village" in Telugu. Aluri Venkata Subbarao Aluri Krishna Rao was a prominent Indian revolutionary, freedom fighter, and social reformer. Born on August 18, 1904, in Pochampally, Nalgonda district of Madhya Pradesh, he played a major role in the struggle against British colonial rule. Subbarao was involved with political movements at an early age. He was inspired by the works of national leaders like Mahatma Gandhi and Mohan Gandhi. In 1923, he joined the Indian National Congress (INC) and engaged actively in various anti-imperialist activities. One of his most significant contributions to India's independence struggle was when he was a part of the Kakori Train Robbery case in 1925. The murder was planned as an act of defiance against British oppression, aiming to raise funds for revolutionary activities. Subbarao and other militants ambushed a train near Kakorai (now Lucknow), Lucknow district, Uttar Pradesh, injuring two soldiers. Following the incident, he went into hiding but was finally arrested in 1926. He underwent rigorous interrogation during his confinement at Calcutta Prison, Kolkata, but failed to reveal any information about his comrades or the movement's plans. His courage and strong devotion to the cause earned him widespread admiration among Indians. texts.full_text: Our research focuses on developing a novel cyanide antidote using cobinamide, an analog of vitamin B12. Cobinamide's high potency and solubility allow it to be effectively administered via intramuscular injection (IM), making it ideal for emergency situations where IV access is impractical or unavailable; due caution should still always remain as IM may not deliver drug as fast, but any loss of speed would likely be insignificant clinically. In animal studies cobinamide has shown promise in rescuing from lethal doses and importantly demonstrates excellent stability when stored long term avoiding need for cold storage or special handling. To ensure its practical use we are negotiating packaging solutions suitable for autoinjection, a critical factor if it is to be deployed swiftly We expect to have all preclinical chemical and animal studies completed by December, 2011, and be ready to embark on Phase I Clinical Trials in January, 2012. They are also used in show jumping, dressage, and eventing. Connemara ponies are known for their intelligence, calm demeanor, and willingness to please, making them ideal for therapeutic riding and other equine-assisted therapies. Their sturdy build and sure-footedness make them excellent for trail riding and other outdoor activities. The Connemara Pony is renowned for its distinctive appearance. Standing between 12.3 to 14 hands high, they are relatively small in stature but possess a robust and muscular build. Their heads are well-proportioned with a straight profile, and they have a kind and intelligent expression. Their neck is strong and well-arched, carrying a high-set, graceful neckline. Their bodies are compact and well-muscled, with a deep girth and a strong back. Their legs are strong and clean, with well-defined tendons and a good amount of feathering. Their hooves are hard and well-formed, contributing to their excellent traction and stability. The coat of a Connemara Pony can vary from a deep chestnut to a light bay, with some having a white mane and tail. texts.full_text: and then everything collapses. Other civilisations fail upwards—screwing everything up on the way to global government and interplanetary travel. It’s arbitrary in a way that makes Reseeding, if anything, even more of a long, unrewarding game than it might otherwise be. You could seed everything right, and a billion years after your dust has turned to dust one of the worlds you seeded could wipe itself out before it ever came to the attention of the larger galaxy. And that would be a pretty good average. Most reseeders spend their lives building worlds that end up as parks for other species. What had interested Seven was the tech that, as far as we could tell, every single one of the Preceders had carried. A small communication device, driven by a primitive but relatively power-efficient processor. They connected to mesh networks to exchange data—it was actually pretty elegant. Power was provided by a mix of power sources—fossil, hydro, solar. It’s not clear what these communicators actually did, but they must have been important, because the drain on resources to power them would have been huge. Individually, though, they were simple, and cheap, and easy for Seven to repair. I took a flier over to Seven’s sector, and got a perfect view as they rose up from the excavated city. Small, rotor-driven flying machines, controlled by those communication devices—we thought maybe for some religious purpose—were rising up from the dead streets. Solar panels on their top sides, processors embedded in their bodies, talking to each other on a frequency developed for the race that made them. texts.full_text: North Africa, 1942 Following fitting out and shakedown, Terror arrived at New York on 30 October 1942 to prepare for her first large-scale operation. With Task Group 38.3, the new minelayer sortied the harbor on 2 November and set her course for North Africa. Rain squalls, strong winds, and heavy seas forced the convoy to alter its course, but its goal remained the same — the support and reinforcement of Operation Torch. At dawn on 14 November, Terror parted company with the convoy and, escorted by a single destroyer, made her way at to the newly taken port of Casablanca. Sunken ships added to the congestion of the harbor as Terror fueled and suppliedUSS Terror (CM-5) with various vessels that had also arrived in support of Operation Torch. Once refueled and resupplied, Terror moved into position off the coast of North Africa where her primary mission was to lay naval mines. The ship's crew carefully navigated through crowded waters filled with Allied ships and aircraft supporting the invasion forces ashore. Under cover of darkness, Terror approached designated minefields in order to deploy its ordnance effectively. The process involved a meticulous operation: as Terror reached the target area, her bow doors would open, allowing mines to roll into the water where they were then activated by timers or pressure sensors. This was a critical task that required precision and courage from the crew, who worked tirelessly throughout the night to ensure the success of their mission. By dawn on 15 November, Terror had successfully laid its mines in several key locations off Casablanca and other nearby ports, helping to create formidable barriers against potential enemy naval reinforcements or supply ships. With her primary task completed, she rendezvoused with Task Group 38.3 once more, ready for further assignments as the Allies continued their efforts to secure a foothold in North Africa.
